comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review advisory triage (PASS + signed, 0 blocking):
- G6 (major) — "add a per-op floor for the agentic verifier instead of raising the GLOBAL default": user-DIRECTED to double the default step budget; the global default is the documented knob (REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS). A per-op floor in the workflow AgentStepRunner is a larger workflow-engine change and out of scope for this directive. Noted as a future option, not adopted.
- G1G2 (minor) — `docs/config.md:197` (`max_steps = 25` + the tool-call approximation): VALID, will refresh to the new default in this change (added to scope).
- G1G2 + T8 (minor) — the `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_review_budget.py` module docstring asserting the default is 25: VALID, will refresh the docstring alongside the assertions.
- E6 (minor) — no AC ties directly to "no more INDETERMINATE from the 25-step default": the failure is LLM-path-dependent (not deterministically testable); covered by the default-value AC + the recorded runtime evidence (c38b: 25→INDETERMINATE, 50→PASS). Acknowledged.
- P6 (minor) — DET ac-quality heuristic; ACs are individually verifiable. Acknowledged.
Scope add from triage: docs/config.md + the test_review_budget.py docstring.

compiled_state.description:
The agent step-budget default is too low for the agentic plan-review VERIFIER, causing routine INDETERMINATE plan reviews on code-grounded tickets.

## Why
`DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS = 25` (env `REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS`, src/rebar/llm/config.py:237) is too low for the agentic verifier (`plan-review-verifier-agentic`), which re-grounds findings against real code with tools. On code-grounded tickets it routinely exceeds 25 steps mid-verify → the `verify` step fails → the gate degrades to INDETERMINATE (`llm_unavailable`) → no signed review → the claim is blocked. Observed this cycle on `tarry-creep-range` (c38b): default 25 → INDETERMINATE; `REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS=50` → PASS + signed. Doubling the default to 50 removes the routine flake while staying well under the review-op floors that still apply.

## What / files
- `src/rebar/llm/config.py` — `DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS` 25 → 50, and refresh the env-doc comment ("25 ~= 12 tool calls" → "50 ~= 25 tool calls").
- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py`, `src/rebar/llm/code_review.py`, `src/rebar/llm/operations.py` — refresh the stale "REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS=25 ≈ 12 tool calls" comments to the new default (the flooring to `_REVIEW_MIN_STEPS=120` / `_VERIFY_MIN_STEPS=240` is unchanged; the "far too low for review" sentiment still holds since 50 < 120).
- `tests/unit/test_config_llm.py` — the default assertion `(16000, 25, 600)` → `(16000, 50, 600)`.
- `tests/interfaces/contracts/test_review_budget.py` — the `cfg.max_iterations == 25` preconditions → `== 50` (the `>= _FLOOR` assertions stay valid since 50 < 120).

## Scope
In: the default value + the stale doc/comment refresh + the two test updates. Out: the per-op floors (`_REVIEW_MIN_STEPS`/`_VERIFY_MIN_STEPS`), `max_tokens`, `timeout`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `DEFAULT_MAX_ITERATIONS` is 50; `LLMConfig.from_env()` with no override yields `max_iterations == 50`.
- [ ] The `REBAR_LLM_MAX_STEPS` env override and the `REBAR_LLM_MAX_ITERS` legacy alias still take precedence over the default (unchanged).
- [ ] No doc/comment still claims the framework default is 25 (the stale "=25 ≈ 12 tool calls" notes are refreshed).
- [ ] The review/verify op floors (`_REVIEW_MIN_STEPS=120`, `_VERIFY_MIN_STEPS=240`) are unchanged and still applied above the new default.
- [ ] `make check` + the suite pass (`test_config_llm` + `test_review_budget` updated).

compiled_state.title: Double the default LLM agent step budget (25 -> 50)
